qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in snapshot chunk loading allows arbitrary file write via SendSnapshot RPC
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding targets unsanitized SnapshotChunk.Name handling in SendSnapshot snapshot loading, while the advisory targets trustedCertPool() in common/security/tls.go parsing only the first PEM block from a trustedCaFile bundle.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated access to all gRPC services including cluster management operations
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding targets default-disabled gRPC authentication and missing handler authorization, while the advisory targets TLS CA bundle parsing in trustedCertPool() and resulting mTLS certificate-chain validation failure.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is specifically about common/security/tls.go trustedCertPool() decoding only one PEM block from trustedCaFile bundles, which breaks mTLS certificate-chain validation. The final qualifying findings instead cover SendSnapshot path traversal and default unauthenticated gRPC operations.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-7jrq-q4pq-rhm6 describes trustedCertPool() in common/security/tls.go calling pem.Decode() only once and discarding later certificates in a CA bundle.
snapshot-path-traversal reports unsanitized SnapshotChunk.Name reaching filepath.Join() and os.OpenFile() in the SendSnapshot snapshot-loading path.
unauthenticated-cluster-operations reports authentication disabled by default and missing authorization checks across Oxia gRPC services.
Neither qualifying report references trustedCertPool(), trustedCaFile, PEM block iteration, corrupted CA-file handling, or the mTLS certificate-chain validation failure described by the advisory.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so runtime tiering is not applicable.
